Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. LDL cholesterol is slightly elevated. Blood count is within normal limits. Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings; consider assessment for underlying metabolic or renal disease.
